Artwork.title: Artwork
Artwork.description: Represents an artwork in the portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Artwork entity.

displayOrder.description: The order in which the artwork is displayed in the gallery.

WebsiteConfiguration.title: WebsiteConfiguration
WebsiteConfiguration.description: Represents the website configuration, including color scheme and other UI settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WebsiteConfiguration entity.

backgroundColor.description: Background color of the website (hex code).

textColor.description: Text color of the website (hex code).

outlineColor.description: Outline color of the website (hex code).

accentColor.description: Accent color for interactive elements (hex code).

PageContent.title: PageContent
PageContent.description: Stores the content for each page of the website (Home, About Me, Contact).

id.description: Unique identifier for the PageContent entity.

content.description: The HTML or text content of the page.

definition.description: Stores the website configuration settings. websiteConfigurationId should have only one value.

params.name: websiteConfigurationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the website configuration document. Should only have one document.

definition.description: Stores content for individual pages (Home, About Me, Contact).

params.name: pageContentId
params.description: Unique identifier for each page content document.

params.name: artworkId
params.description: Unique identifier for each artwork document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a portfolio website, DeepTerrorGG, with a focus on artwork presentation, website configuration, and page content management. To ensure Authorization Independence, ownership, and integrity, the following structure is recommended:

1.  **Website Configuration:** The `websiteConfiguration` collection stores the website's color scheme and UI settings. There's only one document in this collection.
2.  **Page Content:** The `pageContent` collection stores the content for each page of the website (Home, About Me, Contact). Each document represents a page.
3.  **Artwork:** The `artwork` collection stores artwork information, including the image URL, title, description, and display order. There's no user specific data.

This structure promotes Authorization Independence because there are no hierarchical dependencies. Each collection is independent and doesn't rely on data from other collections for authorization. The application uses simple reads, writes and lists, making the QAPs straightforward.
